outbound_official.notes: Official source is the China Ministry of Foreign Affairs Consular Services portal (cs.mfa.gov.cn), section 出国签证信息 (Outbound Visa Information). The authoritative table is '持普通护照中国公民前往有关国家和地区入境便利待遇一览表' (Entry Facilitation Benefits Table for Chinese Ordinary Passport Holders), last updated 2023-12-07 at https://cs.mfa.gov.cn/zggmcg/cgqz/qzxx_660462/202110/t20211030_10404169.shtml (now redirects; PDF archived at https://cs.mfa.gov.cn/zggmcg/cgqz/qzxx_660462/202110/P020231208361019295346.pdf). Supplemented by the Mutual Visa Exemption Agreements Table (中外互免签证协定一览表) updated 2025-04-14 at https://cs.mfa.gov.cn/gyls/lsgz/fwxx/202504/t20250414_11594222.shtml, which is the definitive list of bilateral agreements covering ordinary passport holders. Countries with bilateral agreements added after the 2023 table update (Malaysia, Samoa, Nauru, Micronesia, Uzbekistan, Azerbaijan added 2025) appear in the bilateral table but not yet reflected in the facilitation PDF. The 2023 table is the last known consolidated outbound facilitation list; bilateral agreements page is the current living reference.

regional_policies.name: Hainan Province 30-Day Visa-Free Entry
regional_policies.conditions: Ordinary passport holders from 59 countries; entry/exit via all open ports in Hainan; purposes limited to tourism, business, visits, family visits, medical treatment, conferences/exhibitions, sports competitions. Working and studying excluded. Stay calculated from 00:00 the day after entry.

regional_policies.name: Cruise Ship Tourist Group Visa-Free Entry
regional_policies.conditions: Any foreign tourist group of 2+ individuals organized and received by a China-registered travel agency may enter visa-free via cruise ship through 13 designated cruise ports (Beihai, Dalian, Guangzhou, Haikou, Lianyungang, Qingdao, Sanya, Shanghai, Shenzhen, Tianjin, Wenzhou, Xiamen, Zhoushan). Stay of up to 15 days permitted in Beijing and 11 coastal provinces/municipalities (Fujian, Guangdong, Guangxi, Hainan, Hebei, Jiangsu, Liaoning, Shandong, Shanghai, Tianjin, Zhejiang). Must depart on the same cruise ship.

regional_policies.name: Xishuangbanna (Yunnan) ASEAN Tourist Group Visa-Free Entry
regional_policies.conditions: Tourist groups (2+ individuals, ordinary passports) from any ASEAN member state, organized and received by a China-registered travel agency. Entry/exit via Xishuangbanna Gasa International Airport, Mohan Railway Port, or Mohan Highway Port. Must enter and leave as part of the tour group. Stay within Xishuangbanna Dai Autonomous Prefecture only.

regional_policies.name: Guilin (Guangxi) ASEAN Tourist Group Visa-Free Entry
regional_policies.conditions: Tourist groups (2+ individuals, ordinary passports) from any ASEAN member state, organized and received by a China-registered travel agency. Entry/exit via Guilin airport. Must enter and leave as part of the tour group. Stay within Guilin, Guangxi Zhuang Autonomous Region only.

regional_policies.name: Hong Kong/Macao SAR to Guangdong Province Tourist Group Visa-Free Entry
regional_policies.conditions: Foreign nationals holding ordinary passports from countries with diplomatic relations with China may enter 9 cities in the Guangdong-Hong Kong-Macao Greater Bay Area plus Shantou visa-free, provided they enter Hong Kong or Macao SAR first and then enter Guangdong Province as part of a travel agency tourist group. Cities covered: Dongguan, Foshan, Guangzhou, Huizhou, Jiangmen, Shantou, Shenzhen, Zhaoqing, Zhongshan, Zhuhai. Must enter/exit via ports in these 10 cities.

regional_policies.name: Hong Kong/Macao SAR to Hainan Province Tourist Group Visa-Free Entry
regional_policies.conditions: Foreign nationals holding ordinary passports from countries with diplomatic relations with China may enter Hainan Province visa-free, provided they enter Hong Kong or Macao SAR first and then enter Hainan as part of a travel agency tourist group. Must enter/exit via any open port in Hainan.

visa_policy.notes: China operates a unilateral visa-free policy for ordinary passport holders of 50 countries (30-day max stay). Separately, China has bilateral visa exemption agreements with 158 countries/entities, but most cover only diplomatic/service/official passports. Regional policies include Hainan's 30-day visa-free for 59 countries and shorter regional policies for tourist groups. The 240-hour visa-free transit covers 55 nationalities at 65 designated ports. Most unilateral policies are time-limited (mostly until Dec 31, 2026; Russia extended to Dec 31, 2027; Brunei has no stated expiry). No visa on arrival is offered. Stays calculated from 00:00 the day after entry. NIA page last updated 2026-02-17.

visa_types.name: L Visa (Tourist)
visa_types.purpose: Tourism, family visits, transit with sightseeing
visa_types.notes: Standard tourist visa. Single-entry most common; double/multiple entry available at embassy discretion. 30-day stay per entry typical. Applied at Chinese embassy/consulate or CVASC (China Visa Application Service Center). Fee varies by nationality (reciprocity-based).

visa_types.name: M Visa (Business/Trade)
visa_types.notes: For commercial/trade activities. Multiple-entry common for business travelers. Invitation letter from Chinese business partner typically required.

visa_types.name: X1/X2 Visa (Student)
visa_types.notes: X1 for study programs 180+ days (must convert to residence permit in China); X2 for programs under 180 days. Requires JW201/JW202 form and admission letter from recognized Chinese educational institution.

visa_types.name: Z Visa (Work)
visa_types.notes: Single-entry; must be converted to a work-type Residence Permit within 30 days of arrival. Requires work permit notification letter issued by Chinese authorities and employment contract. Sponsored by Chinese employer.

visa_types.name: G Visa (Transit)
visa_types.notes: For travelers transiting China. Note: nationals of 53 countries qualify for Visa-Free Transit (72/144 hours) at designated ports without a G visa — check official list. G visa required for those who do not qualify for visa-free transit or who will exceed the free transit window.

credential.subtype: APEC Business Travel Card (with 'CHN' listed on back as visa-free economy)

conditional_access.conditions: Holder must present a valid APEC Business Travel Card (ABTC) with 'CHN' printed on the back as one of the visa-free APEC economies, together with a valid passport. Card must be within its 5-year validity period. Entry permitted for business purposes only. If stay needs to exceed 60 days for medical or business emergency, a stay permit extension must be requested from local public security exit-entry department at least 7 days before expiry of 60-day period; extension granted for maximum 30 days if deemed reasonable. Eligible issuing economies: all APEC participating economies whose ABTCs list CHN (excludes US and Canada which are observer members without visa function for China). Access to special APEC border inspection passages at designated ports.

China has bilateral mutual visa exemption agreements with 158 countries/entities covering diplomatic and/or service/official passport holders. Max stay is typically 30 days per entry; some agreements allow 90 days (e.g. Vietnam, Mexico, Mali, Rwanda, Bahrain, Burkina Faso, El Salvador, Madagascar, Lebanon); unlimited stays for specific passport types under agreements with Bolivia, Mongolia, Turkey, Laos, Bangladesh. A small number of agreements also cover ordinary passports (e.g. Mauritius, Seychelles, Fiji, Georgia, Solomon Islands, Antigua and Barbuda, San Marino, UK, Mexico, Vietnam). Exact passport types and stay limits vary by bilateral agreement — full list at cs.mfa.gov.cn.

cbi.notes: China has no citizenship-by-investment program. The Nationality Law of the People's Republic of China (Article 7) provides for naturalization of foreigners who are 'near relatives of Chinese nationals', have 'settled in China', or have 'other legitimate reasons' — no investment pathway is mentioned. China does not recognize dual nationality (Article 3). All naturalization applications are subject to approval by the Ministry of Public Security. Source: https://en.nia.gov.cn/n147418/n147458/c155976/content.html

programs.name: Permanent Residence by Foreign Investment (General)
programs.notes: Foreign nationals who make direct and stable investments of over USD 2 million in China with good tax payment records for three consecutive years may apply for permanent residence. Permanent residence card valid 5–10 years; renewable. Application fee: ¥1,500 + ¥300 card production. Processing time: up to 6 months. No official path-to-citizenship timeline stated under this program.

programs.name: Permanent Residence by Foreign Investment (Central China)
programs.notes: Reduced threshold of USD 1 million for investments in the central part of China. Same 3-year stable investment and good tax record requirement.

programs.name: Permanent Residence by Foreign Investment (Western / Poverty-Relief Counties / Encouraged Industries)
programs.notes: Reduced threshold of USD 500,000 for investments in western China, key national poverty alleviation counties, or industries listed in the Catalogue of Encouraged Industries. Same 3-year stable investment and good tax record requirement.

programs.name: Permanent Residence for High-Level Talent / Senior Professionals
programs.notes: Holders of deputy general manager roles or equivalent senior professional titles (e.g., associate professor) who have received equivalent salary in China for at least 4 consecutive years and resided in China for a total of not less than 3 years within those 4 years, with good tax records, are eligible for permanent residence.

programs.name: R Visa (High-Level Talent Visa)
programs.eligibility: Foreigners classified as 'high-level talents or whose skills are urgently needed in China'; must obtain Certification documents confirming qualifications from competent Chinese government authorities and a Confirmation Letter for High-level Foreign Talents from an inviting organization.
programs.notes: China's dedicated visa category for high-level foreign talent. Offers more favorable terms than standard work visas. Application hotline: 12367.

programs.name: K Visa (Young Science and Technology Professionals)
programs.eligibility: Young science and technology professionals meeting qualifications set by relevant Chinese authorities. No mandatory domestic employer or invitation letter required, unlike other visa types.
programs.processing_time: Streamlined application process; specific days not stated on official page
programs.notes: New visa category effective October 1, 2025. Offers more convenience in number of permitted entries, validity period, and duration of stay compared to China's 12 ordinary visa types. Holders may participate in education, culture, science and technology exchanges, as well as entrepreneurial and business activities.

programs.name: Talent Endorsement Policy (Multiple-Exit-Entry)
programs.eligibility: Outstanding talents, scientific research talents, cultural and educational talents, healthcare and medical talents, legal talents, and other talents working in Beijing-Tianjin-Hebei, Yangtze River Delta, Guangdong-Hong Kong-Macao Greater Bay Area, all pilot free trade zones nationwide. Requires relevant certification from competent talent authorities.
programs.notes: Effective November 5, 2025, expanded from 3 initial regions to include entire Yangtze River Delta, Beijing-Tianjin-Hebei, and all pilot free trade zones. Grants multiple-exit-entry endorsement to/from Hong Kong SAR or Macao SAR with validity of 1–5 years; 30 days max stay per visit. In Hetao Shenzhen-Hong Kong Science and Technology Innovation Cooperation Zone, 3-year endorsements may be obtained without talent certificates.

data_quality.validation_notes: Validated 2026-06-13 against live official sources. Key corrections made vs prior record: (1) Russia visa-free expiry corrected from 'September 14, 2026' to 'December 31, 2027' — per MFA spokesperson Guo Jiakun announcement on May 20, 2026 (source: english.www.gov.cn). (2) Corrected visa_free country count in notes from '51 countries' to '50 countries', matching official NIA page (dated 2026-02-17) which lists exactly 50: 35 European, 2 Oceania, 7 Asian, 6 Americas. (3) The Hainan Province group entry has been moved from visa_policy.visa_free to the new visa_policy.regional_policies array where it is properly classified as a regional conditional policy (not a unilateral nationality-based waiver). (4) The 240-hour transit eta entry country list has been corrected: Mexico (MEX) was missing from the South America group — NIA page (dated 2025-07-04) lists 4 South American countries: Argentina, Brazil, Chile, Mexico. (5) Added 5 additional regional policies now documented on the NIA regional policies page (cruise ship groups, Xishuangbanna ASEAN, Guilin ASEAN, Guangdong via HK/Macao, Hainan via HK/Macao). (6) Added english.www.gov.cn/news/202605/21 and english.www.gov.cn/news/202509/02 to source_urls.
gaps: Full enumerated list of all 158 bilateral mutual visa exemption agreement countries with exact passport types and max stay days was not scraped in full detail — the cs.mfa.gov.cn list page was not re-scraped in this validation run. The NIA summary page confirms 158 agreements exist.
E-visa system eligibility list by nationality was not enumerated from official source. The official portal (evisa.mofa.gov.cn) was not directly scraped.
Specific validity period and exact stay duration granted by the R visa was not found verbatim on official pages.
Path-to-citizenship timeline from permanent residence is not stated on official sources — China's Nationality Law does not specify a fixed residency period for naturalization.

No official Chinese government source confirms any visa-free / VoA / e-visa rule based on holding a foreign (US, Schengen, UK, Canada, Australia, GCC) visa or residence permit/permanent resident card. China does NOT grant conditional entry based on third-country visas or residence permits.
No OCI (Overseas Citizen of India) cardholder exemption rule found on any official Chinese government source.
Exact list of APEC economies whose ABTCs list 'CHN' as a visa-free destination could not be confirmed from a directly-scraped NIA page.

Russia's extension announcement was confirmed via english.www.gov.cn summary; direct MFA press conference transcript (mfa.gov.cn) returned maintenance page during validation — the extension fact is corroborated by multiple secondary sources and the official government news summary.
